title: quercus longinux var. kuoi
intro: Quercus longinux var. kuoi, commonly known as Kuoi Oak or Taiwan Oak, is a variant of the Quercus longinux tree species native to Taiwan. The Kuoi Oak is valued for its rich source of antioxidants and flavonoids, which have been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant's leaves and bark have been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and have been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. Kuoi Oak is typically prepared as a decoction or tea, and its herbal preparations are also used in modern medicine to support immune function and overall well-being.
